title: Minor burns - aftercare
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Minor burns can involve redness, pain, and small blisters when superficial.
Care can include cooling the burn, gentle cleaning, protection, and avoiding further injury.
Patients should avoid breaking blisters or applying unsafe substances.
Large, deep, circumferential, chemical/electrical, inhalation-associated, infected, face/hand/genital, pediatric, pregnancy, or high-risk-host burns require separate clinician-directed care.
